exercises.prompt: Complete: « Si nous consommions moins, nous ___ l'eau. »
exercises.explanation: Si + imparfait → conditionnel présent: nous conserverions.

exercises.prompt: Express a regret: « J'___ écouter tes conseils. »
exercises.explanation: Regret about the past → conditionnel passé: j'aurais dû (I should have).

exercises.template: À ta place, je {{b1}} le train.
exercises.explanation: An imagined situation (if I were you) → conditionnel présent: je prendrais.

exercises.template: Selon la presse, la France {{b1}} bientôt 37 kg de déchets par jour.
exercises.explanation: Journalists use the conditional for unconfirmed claims: produirait = reportedly will produce.

exercises.prompt: Complete: « Si ça continue comme ça, il n'y ___ plus de forêts. »
exercises.explanation: Si + présent → futur: si ça continue, il n'y aura plus… (Never future directly after si!)

exercises.prompt: Replace « à ce projet » with the right pronoun (one word).
exercises.template: — Tu crois à ce projet ? — Oui, j'{{b1}} crois.
exercises.explanation: Croire À quelque chose → y: j'y crois.

exercises.prompt: Complete: « — Tu manges de la viande ? — Non, je n'___ mange jamais. »
exercises.explanation: De la viande (partitive) → en: je n'en mange jamais.

exercises.prompt: Match each expression to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: "If I were you, I would take the bike."
Listen to the hypothesis. What does it say?
exercises.transcript: Si on plantait plus d'arbres, les villes seraient moins chaudes en été.

options.text: If we planted more trees, cities would be cooler in summer
We planted trees, so cities are cooler now
Cities will plant trees next summer

exercises.explanation: Si + imparfait (plantait) → conditionnel (seraient): an imagined situation, not a fact.

exercises.prompt: Read the eco-debate exchange and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: AMANDINE : Si on avait agi il y a dix ans, on aurait évité la disparition de plusieurs espèces. LÉO : Tu crois vraiment à ces programmes de protection ? Moi, j'en doute un peu. AMANDINE : Moi, j'y crois ! Et à ta place, je m'informerais avant de douter.
exercises.passageEn: AMANDINE: If we had acted ten years ago, we would have avoided the extinction of several species. LÉO: Do you really believe in these protection programmes? I have my doubts. AMANDINE: I believe in them! And if I were you, I'd read up before doubting.

questions.prompt: What does Amandine say about acting ten years ago?

options.text: It would have avoided the extinction of several species
It was impossible to act back then
Several species were saved ten years ago

questions.prompt: What is Léo's attitude towards the protection programmes?

exercises.prompt: Hindsight hypothesis: « Si on avait agi plus tôt, on ___ la crise. »
exercises.explanation: Si + plus-que-parfait → conditionnel passé: on aurait évité.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Si nous consommions moins, nous conserverions l'eau.

options.text: If we consumed less, we would conserve water.
If you don't believe me, read this article!
You should take your bike more often.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.explanation: « Faisons une hypothèse simple. » — a hypothesis.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: “If we consumed less, we would conserve water.”
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Si nous consommions moins, nous conserverions l'eau.
exercises.targetEn: If we consumed less, we would conserve water.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Tu devrais prendre le vélo plus souvent.
exercises.targetEn: You should take your bike more often.
